# Transcrição: The_Importance_of_Refinement

- **Fonte:** The_Importance_of_Refinement
- **Duração:** 01:10
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:10]

My question is, after practising the same speech a hundred times, I got so bored myself. What do we do? Do we just... Right, that's a really good question because as a keynote speaker, the reason keynote speakers, not just me but many other keynote speakers, the reason they get paid where they get paid is not because it's a keynote they wrote yesterday. Adele doesn't get paid a million dollars to sing a four-minute song on stage because she wrote the song yesterday and started singing it four days before that. Adele gets paid that money because she's been singing that song for the last seven years. Right? So there is something to refinement. So the speech gets boring when we no longer refine the speech. A stage workshop 18 right now you're all a part of is continually being refined. I have to start refining things and if you continually start refining things, it always stays interesting. But if you keep it exactly the same, then you're right. We're human, it will get boring. So I say adopt the philosophy of kaizen, which is a Japanese philosophy of continuous improvement. I think that is what makes it exciting for me and I hope that helps you on your journey. Keep adding new things, make it better and it stays exciting.
